title: Employment Law Expert Witness | Psychology Direct
description: Expert psychological and psychiatric assessments for employment tribunals. Workplace stress, bullying, discrimination, and occupational health.

blocks.headline: Win Employment Tribunal Cases with Expert Psychological Evidence
blocks.subtitle: We match you with occupational psychologists and psychiatrists who understand employment law. You get detailed reports on workplace harm, causation, and prognosis—ready for tribunal and Legal Aid rates included.

blocks.heading: Expert Services for Employment Tribunal Claims

children.text: Occupational psychologists assess psychological harm from workplace conduct. They evaluate bullying, stress, discrimination trauma, and functional impairment. Most importantly, they show the clear link between what happened at work and the claimant's mental health impact.
Workplace stress and stress-related disorders assessment
Occupational health and fitness to work evaluation
Causation opinions (linking workplace conduct to harm)
Psychiatrists provide medical opinion on mental health conditions from workplace harm. They diagnose conditions, explain causation, recommend treatment, and assess prognosis for tribunal evidence.
Mental health diagnosis secondary to workplace harm
Depression and anxiety secondary to workplace conduct

Employment tribunal cases hinge on proving workplace conduct caused psychological harm. Our experts understand employment law, workplace dynamics, and occupational health. They assess three key questions: Was the harm foreseeable? Did the employer fall below the standard of care? Is there a clear causal link? We match you with an expert whose employment law background fits your case.
Not sure? Use our tool: Psychologist or Psychiatrist? → /tools/psychologist-or-psychiatrist/

steps.title: Brief Us on the Case
steps.description: Call us on 01306 879 975 with details of the workplace conduct, the claimant's response, and your tribunal timeline.

steps.title: Receive Matched Experts
steps.description: Within 24 hours, we send 2–3 CVs from occupational psychologists or psychiatrists with employment tribunal experience.

steps.title: Instruct & We Handle Logistics
steps.description: We arrange appointments, coordinate with employers, and manage all administration. You stay in control.

steps.title: Get Your Tribunal Report
steps.description: The expert delivers a detailed report on harm, causation, and prognosis. We quality-check everything. Standard turnaround 4–6 weeks; fast-track available.

rows.col1: Same or less than instructing directly

faqs.question: What is the test for workplace stress claims?
faqs.answer: Employers owe a duty of care to employees. If an employer knew (or should have known) that workplace conduct was causing psychological harm, and failed to prevent it, they may be liable. Expert evidence on foreseeability and causation is critical.

faqs.question: Do I need a psychologist or a psychiatrist?
faqs.answer: Occupational psychologists are best for stress, bullying, and discrimination assessment. Psychiatrists are helpful when diagnosed conditions need medical opinion or medication is involved. Many cases benefit from both. Use our free tool to check: Psychologist or Psychiatrist? → /tools/psychologist-or-psychiatrist/

faqs.question: How do experts assess causation?
faqs.answer: Experts assess three things: Was the harm foreseeable? Did the employer fall below the standard of care? Is there a clear link between the workplace conduct and the claimant's psychological condition? They also consider alternative causes.

faqs.question: How long does a tribunal report take?
faqs.answer: Standard turnaround is 4–6 weeks. We offer fast-track services for urgent dates. Contact us on 01306 879 975 to discuss your timeline.

faqs.question: What if the claimant is still in employment?
faqs.answer: Assessments work either way — during employment or after departure. The expert will advise on optimal timing for your specific case and any impact on findings.

faqs.question: How much does an employment tribunal expert cost?
faqs.answer: Costs vary by case complexity and assessment scope. Use our fee calculator for an instant estimate, or call 01306 879 975. For Legal Aid cases, we work at LAA rates.

faqs.question: Will the expert attend the tribunal?
faqs.answer: Reports stand alone for tribunal purposes. If you need direct evidence, the expert can be instructed separately for tribunal attendance. Discuss this with your Client Manager.

blocks.heading: Ready to Instruct Your Expert?
blocks.bodyText: We'll match you with an expert who knows employment tribunals and handle everything from briefing to report delivery.
